facts.text: George Stypmann built the island’s first home in 1872, naming it Jupiter House and sparking modern settlement.
A Spanish explorer christened the island Santa Lucia, honoring the patron saint of vision after spotting its stunning landscape.
The Jupiter Inlet Lifesaving Station opened in 1892 to aid mariners through the island’s treacherous waters.
The lighthouse, erected in 1860, remains an active aid to navigation and a historic landmark on Jupiter Island.
The Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area, established in 2008, protects over 120 acres of maritime hammock, mangrove swamp, and scrub habitat.
Jupiter Island is a picturesque barrier island nestled between the Indian River Lagoon and the Atlantic Ocean on Florida’s Treasure Coast.
